zh.Harewood Castle, Leeds, West Yorkshire,: 英國英格蘭越可爾希亞活 卡素
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Redman I於大約1360年出生在英國越可爾時，他的父親Matthew III Redman71歲，母親Alice Greystocke59歲。他於1393年9月7日與Elizabeth Aldeburgh在英國越可爾希亞活哥吉拉 沙智 邱奇結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1403年被列為high sheriff of yorkshire。他去世於1426年5月22日在英國越可爾希亞活 卡素享年67歲，埋葬在英國越可爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Redman I于大约1360年出生在英国越可爾时，他的父亲Matthew III Redman71岁，母亲Alice Greystocke59岁。他于1393年9月7日与Elizabeth Aldeburgh在英国越可爾希亞活哥吉拉 沙智 邱奇结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1403年被列为high sheriff of yorkshire。他去世于1426年5月22日在英国越可爾希亞活 卡素享年67岁，埋葬在英国越可爾。

nameMeanings.name: Redman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English red (Old English hrēod) ‘reed’ + -man, presumably for a thatcher or reed-cutter. Compare Reeder .
English: in southeastern England sometimes a topographic name perhaps for someone who lived by a clearing, from Middle English rede (Old English rēod, rȳd) ‘clearing’ + man.
English: habitational name from Redmain (Cumberland), from Old English rēad ‘red’ + an uncertain second element, perhaps a dialect form of man ‘cairn’.

variants.name: Reeder
meanings: English: occupational name for someone who used reeds as a roofing material (a thatcher), a derivative of Middle English rede ‘reed’ + -er. Compare Reed and Redman .

English: topographic name for someone who lived by a clearing, a derivative of Middle English rede, ride, rude ‘clearing’ (see Reed ) + -er, synonymous and interchangeable with atte Rede, atte Rude ‘at the clearing’. This type of name, composed of a topographic term + -er, is common in Kent, Surrey and Sussex, and it is also found in Essex.
Dutch and German: variant of   Reder   3.

variants.name: Redhead
meanings: nickname for someone with red hair, from Middle English red ‘red’ + he(ve)d ‘head’. Compare Redman .
habitational name from an unidentified place in northern England called Redhead, probably meaning ‘red headland’.

variants.name: Rodman
meanings: English: occupational name for a thatcher or reed-cutter, from Southwest Middle English rod(e), rud(e) (Old English hrēod) ‘reed’ + -man. Synonymous with Redman 1.
English: topographic name for someone who lived at a clearing, from Middle English rode (Old English rodu) or Southwest Middle English rude (Old English rȳde) ‘clearing’ + man. See Redman 2.
Americanized form of German Rodmann: variant of Rodemann (see Rodeman ).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Redman I was born about 1360, in Yorkshire, England, his father, Matthew III Redman, was 71 and his mother, Alice Greystocke, was 59. He married Elizabeth Aldeburgh on 7 September 1393, in All Saints Church, Harewood, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. In 1403, at the age of 44, his occupation is listed as high sheriff of yorkshire. He died on 22 May 1426, in Harewood Castle, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 67, and was buried in Yorkshire, England.

fr.All Saints Church, Harewood, Yorkshire,: All Saints Church, Harewood, Everwyck, Angleterre
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Redman I est né environ 1360, à Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Matthew III Redman, avait 71 ans et sa mère, Alice Greystocke, avait 59 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Aldeburgh le 7 septembre 1393, à All Saints Church, Harewood, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. En 1403, à l’âge de 44 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est high sheriff of yorkshire. Il est décédé le 22 mai 1426, à Harewood Castle, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Redman I um 1360 in Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Matthew III Redman 71 und seine Mutter Alice Greystocke 59. Er hat am 7. September 1393 in All Saints Church, Harewood, Yorkshire, England Elizabeth Aldeburgh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. 1403, als er 44 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als high sheriff of yorkshire aufgeführt. Er starb am 22. Mai 1426 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Harewood Castle, Yorkshire, England und wurde in Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Redman I è nato il circa 1360 nel Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Matthew III Redman, aveva 71 anni e il suo madre, Alice Greystocke, aveva 59 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Aldeburgh il 7 settembre 1393, nel All Saints Church, Harewood, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Nel 1403, all'età di 44 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come high sheriff of yorkshire. Morì il 22 maggio 1426, nel Harewood Castle, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolto a Yorkshire, England.

ja.Harewood Castle, Leeds, West Yorkshire,: イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアハーエウッド カストル
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Redman Iが約1360年にイギリスヨークシュアイアで生まれた時，父Matthew III Redmanは71歳，母Alice Greystockeは59歳であった。1393年9月7日にイギリスヨークシュアイアハーエウッドアル サインツ チャーチでElizabeth Aldeburghと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。1403年，44歳の時，職業はhigh sheriff of yorkshireと記録されている。彼は1426年5月22日にイギリスヨークシュアイアハーエウッド カストルで67歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.All Saints Church, Harewood, Yorkshire,: 영국 요르크실르 하레우드 앨 세인츠 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Redman I(이)가 대략 1360년 영국 요르크실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Matthew III Redman(은)는 71세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Greystocke(은)는 59세였다. 그는 1393년 9월 7일 영국 요르크실르 하레우드 앨 세인츠 처르치에서 Elizabeth Aldeburgh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1403년, 44세의 나이로 그의 직업은 high sheriff of yorkshire이었다. 그는 1426년 5월 22일 영국 요르크실르 하레우드 카스틀에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Redman I nasceu aproximadamente 1360, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Matthew III Redman, tinha 71 anos e sua mãe, Alice Greystocke, tinha 59 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Aldeburgh em 7 de setembro de 1393, em All Saints Church, Harewood, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Em 1403, com 44 anos, sua profissão aparece como high sheriff of yorkshire. Ele faleceu em 22 de maio de 1426, em Harewood Castle, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 67 anos, e foi sepultado em Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.All Saints Church, Harewood, Yorkshire,: алл сеинц чурч, харюуд, еркшир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Redman I родился около 1360 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Matthew III Redman было 71 год, а матери, которую звали Alice Greystocke, 59. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Aldeburgh 7 сентября 1393 г. в местности алл сеинц чурч, харюуд, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. В 1403 году, в возрасте 44 лет,  он работал по профессии high sheriff of yorkshire. Он умер 22 мая 1426 г. в местности харюуд кастл, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Redman I nació aproximadamente 1360, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Matthew III Redman, tenía 71 años y su mamá, Alice Greystocke, tenía 59 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Aldeburgh el 7 de septiembre de 1393, en All Saints Church, Harewood, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. En 1403, a la edad de 44 años, su ocupación registrada era high sheriff of yorkshire. Murió el 22 de mayo de 1426, en Harewood Castle, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultado en Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
